methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $53M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Biofire, a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Kai Kloepfer, has raised $52.9M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable set of eight private-market peers in Defense & Security at Series A and Series B stages, supplemented by two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Key private peers include NODA AI, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods are applied. The Stage-Progression method, treating total capital raised ($53M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next stage, implies $106M. The Peer-Median method anchors to the median valuation of eight same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which stands at $1.0B (with a range of $125M to $3.0B). This peer cohort includes Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $90M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $553M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-adjusted capital multiples versus peer-based benchmarks. Key risk factors include the nascency of the Defense & Security frontier-tech segment and variability in peer monetization trajectories, which widen the confidence interval considerably relative to more mature verticals.
